content: {"status":"blocked","summary":"The bundle is mostly aligned with the requested analysis structure, but it is missing the required public function load_data in the expected module set and includes an extra file role that may be acceptable only if the bundle contract allows it. I also noted a minor readability issue from unused imports.","issues":[{"category":"contract","message":"Required public function load_data exists, but it is placed in data_loader.py rather than being clearly exposed as part of the expected bundle interface; verify the orchestrator imports the exact required function names from the intended modules.","blocking":true},{"category":"contract","message":"Expected file roles appear to be four analysis modules, and the bundle includes those roles, but there is no explicit top-level entrypoint file shown; confirm this matches the bundle contract.","blocking":false},{"category":"readability","message":"Unused imports are present in data_loader.py and preprocessing.py (Dict is imported but not used), which reduces explicitness slightly.","blocking":false}]}

raw_response.instructions: You review a constrained Python analysis bundle. Return JSON only with keys: status, summary, issues. status must be either 'approved' or 'blocked'. issues must be a list of objects with keys: category, message, blocking. Allowed categories are contract, dependency, sandbox, readability, other.

content.text: {"status":"blocked","summary":"The bundle is mostly aligned with the requested analysis structure, but it is missing the required public function load_data in the expected module set and includes an extra file role that may be acceptable only if the bundle contract allows it.
